message:
feat(bytes): Implement proper PyBytes type system for Python bytes literals

Add comprehensive PyBytes wrapper type to preserve type information between
Python bytes literals and Zig code, enabling correct repr() output (b'...')
and proper binary operations.

Changes:
- Add PyBytes struct with concat, repeat, sliceRange, get operations
- Update type inference for bytes literals, memoryview(), bytes(), bytearray()
- Add bytes + bytes → PyBytes.concat() codegen
- Add bytes * int / int * bytes → PyBytes.repeat() codegen
- Add bytes[start:end] → PyBytes.sliceRange() codegen
- Add bytes[i] → PyBytes.get() codegen
- Update float() builtin to parse PyBytes.data
- Update struct.unpack to handle PyBytes.data
- Update reversed() to wrap result in PyBytes for bytes input
- Add .bytes variant to PyValue union with proper switch handling

files.patch:
@@ -8,6 +8,7 @@ pub const PyValue = union(enum) {
     int: i64,
     float: f64,
     string: []const u8,
+    bytes: @import("../runtime/builtins.zig").PyBytes, // Python bytes type
     bool: bool,
     none: void,
     list: []const PyValue,
@@ -25,6 +26,7 @@ pub const PyValue = union(enum) {
             .int => |v| try writer.print("{d}", .{v}),
             .float => |v| try writer.print("{d}", .{v}),
             .string => |v| try writer.print("{s}", .{v}),
+            .bytes => |v| try writer.print("{s}", .{v.data}),
             .bool => |v| try writer.print("{}", .{v}),
             .none => try writer.writeAll("None"),
             .list => |items| {

@@ -635,7 +635,7 @@ fn inferConstant(value: ast.Value) InferError!NativeType {
         .bigint => .bigint, // Large integers are BigInt
         .float => .float,
         .string => .{ .string = .literal }, // String literals are compile-time constants
-        .bytes => .bytes, // Bytes literals use PyBytes wrapper for type preservation
+        .bytes => .bytes, // Bytes literals use PyBytes wrapper
         .bool => .bool,
         .none => .none,
         .complex => .complex, // Complex number literals
